anchored_threads.thread_name: Sāṅkhya / first-principles knowledge — engineering: APIs vs internal representations, protocols vs wire formats, abstractions vs leakage
anchored_threads.evidence: The verse is the chapter's most explicit statement of the imperishable substrate. The engineering layer maps it to the protocol/contract that pervades every implementation — exactly the chapter-thesis-named domain mapping ('Protocols vs. their wire formats. Abstractions vs. their leakage'). The Hyrum's Law counter-example handles the 'leakage' case explicitly. Block-B sequencing: 2.16 cuts; 2.17 names; 2.18 licenses action; 2.19 negates agency; 2.20 names as ancient. 2.17's role as 'name the substrate' is fulfilled.

verdict_reason: The verse anchors thread 1 of the chapter. The voice is didactic-corrective. The hard edge is preserved (the chapter thesis explicitly says 'the interface survives even when the implementation churns. Do not flatten this into embrace change' — the engineering layer respects this). The verse's role in block B is honored. PASS.
